name: Body Mass Index Calculator
description: Industrial-grade BMI calculator with health risk assessment, lean body mass estimation, and actionable wellness insights based on ISO 8996 and Six Sigma health metrics.

inputs.label: Weight (kg)
inputs.businessContext: Body weight measured in kilograms, per ISO 8996 anthropometric standards.

businessContext_i18n.en: Body weight measured in kilograms, per ISO 8996 anthropometric standards.

inputs.label: Height (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Standing height in centimeters, measured per ISO 7250 ergonomic guidelines.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standing height in centimeters, measured per ISO 7250 ergonomic guidelines.

inputs.label: Age (years)
inputs.businessContext: Chronological age used for metabolic risk adjustment, per Six Sigma health screening protocols.

businessContext_i18n.en: Chronological age used for metabolic risk adjustment, per Six Sigma health screening protocols.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Biological sex for lean body mass and risk stratification, per WHO standards.

businessContext_i18n.en: Biological sex for lean body mass and risk stratification, per WHO standards.

inputs.label: Physical Activity Level
inputs.businessContext: Self-reported activity level for energy expenditure estimation, per ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

businessContext_i18n.en: Self-reported activity level for energy expenditure estimation, per ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

inputs.label: Waist Circumference (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Waist measurement for central obesity risk assessment, per IDF and Lean Six Sigma health metrics.

businessContext_i18n.en: Waist measurement for central obesity risk assessment, per IDF and Lean Six Sigma health metrics.

rules.message: Extreme height-to-weight ratio detected. Verify measurements for accuracy.
High waist circumference in older age group indicates elevated cardiovascular risk.

thresholds.message: Underweight: Increased risk of nutritional deficiencies and osteoporosis.
Overweight: Moderate risk of metabolic syndrome.
Obese Class I: High risk of cardiovascular disease.
Obese Class II: Very high risk. Immediate intervention recommended.
Obese Class III (Severe): Extreme risk. Medical consultation required.
High waist circumference in male: Increased visceral fat risk.
High waist circumference in female: Increased visceral fat risk.

formulas.name: Body Mass Index
formulas.description: Standard BMI calculation per WHO and ISO 8996.

formulas.name: Lean Body Mass (Boer Formula)
formulas.description: Estimated lean body mass using Boer formula, per clinical nutrition standards.

formulas.name: Body Fat Percentage (Deurenberg Formula)
formulas.description: Estimated body fat percentage adjusted for age and gender, per Six Sigma health analytics.

formulas.name: Basal Metabolic Rate (Mifflin-St Jeor)
formulas.description: Resting energy expenditure estimation per ISO 8996 metabolic rate standards.
formulas.formula: gender == 'male' ? (10 * weight_kg + 6.25 * height_cm - 5 * age_years + 5) : (10 * weight_kg + 6.25 * height_cm - 5 * age_years - 161)

formulas.name: Total Daily Energy Expenditure
formulas.description: TDEE adjusted for activity level, per Lean Six Sigma energy balance models.

formulas.name: Waist-to-Height Ratio
formulas.description: Central obesity indicator per WERC and IDF guidelines.

formulas.name: Composite Health Risk Score
formulas.description: Aggregate risk score based on BMI, waist-to-height ratio, and age, using Six Sigma weighted scoring.

primary.label: Body Mass Index
primary.description: Primary BMI result used for weight classification.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Visceral Fat Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High waist-to-height ratio indicates hidden visceral fat, a key driver of metabolic loss.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Age-Related Metabolic Decline
hiddenLossDrivers.description: BMR decreases with age, leading to potential energy imbalance if diet unchanged.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Muscle Mass Deficit
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Low lean body mass relative to weight suggests sarcopenia risk, reducing functional capacity.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Physical Activity
suggestedActions.description: Aim for at least 150 minutes of moderate aerobic activity per week per WHO guidelines.

suggestedActions.label: Dietary Adjustment
suggestedActions.description: Reduce caloric intake by 500 kcal/day if overweight, focusing on protein and fiber.

suggestedActions.label: Strength Training
suggestedActions.description: Incorporate resistance training 2-3 times per week to preserve lean body mass.

suggestedActions.label: Monitor Waist Circumference
suggestedActions.description: Track waist circumference monthly to assess central obesity trends.

suggestedActions.label: Medical Consultation
suggestedActions.description: If health risk score > 5, consult a healthcare provider for comprehensive assessment.
